channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global Geopolitical News Headlines - Curated from Tier 1 Sources

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran responds to US ceasefire proposal as Gulf states report drone attacks
item.description: Tehran has sent a response to Washington via mediator Pakistan regarding a 30-day ceasefire extension and Strait of Hormuz reopening, while drone strikes hit cargo ships off Qatar and were repelled by Kuwait and UAE. [US-Iran War]

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin says Ukraine conflict is coming to an end
item.description: The Russian leader expressed optimism about potential Ukraine negotiations but criticized Western backing for President Zelensky, while aides cautioned it remains a long road to peace. [Ukraine-Russia War]

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel escalates Lebanon strikes, killing 39 despite ceasefire announcement
item.description: Israeli attacks on Lebanon have killed at least 39 people, including an infant, as Israel and Hezbollah continue trading fire despite a ceasefire deal announced last month. [Middle East]

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan car bomb attack kills 15 police officers
item.description: A suicide bomber rammed an explosives-laden vehicle into a checkpoint in Pakistan late Saturday night, killing 15 police officers in one of the deadliest attacks on security forces this year. [South Asia]

item.title: 🇨🇩 DR Congo militia attack kills at least 69 civilians
item.description: CODECO militia fighters killed at least 69 people in an attack in Ituri province, northeastern Democratic Republic of Congo, in one of the deadliest massacres in the region this year. [Africa]

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin leads scaled-down Victory Day parade, vows victory in Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin declared confidence in victory during Moscow's Victory Day parade, which for the first time in nearly two decades featured no tanks or heavy military equipment. Security was tight amid a US-brokered ceasefire. [Ukraine-Russia War]

item.title: 🚢 WHO chief travels to Tenerife for hantavirus cruise ship evacuation
item.description: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus arrived in Spain to oversee the evacuation of over 140 passengers and crew from a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship heading to the Canary Islands. Three people have died and five are infected in the outbreak. [Health]

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump feuds with allies likely to outlast Iran war, analysts warn
item.description: Trump's words and deeds have revived fears among Washington's long-standing friends that the United States might be unreliable in a future crisis, with diplomatic tensions expected to persist even after the Iran conflict ends. [Diplomacy]

item.title: 🇨🇳💻 China unveils worlds first dual-core quantum computer
item.description: Chinas Hanyuan-2 quantum computer operates at room temperature, marking a breakthrough in quantum computing accessibility and maintenance. [Technology]

item.title: 🇯🇵🇳🇿 Japans Mogami-class warship gaining ground in New Zealand defense competition
item.description: Japans Mogami frigate and Britains Type 31 are top contenders for New Zealands naval modernization, with Australia potentially influencing the outcome. [Asia-Pacific]

item.title: 🇺🇦🤖 Ukraine deploys AI-powered turrets against fiber-optic drones
item.description: Ukrainian forces successfully deployed autonomous AI-driven turrets capable of intercepting Russian drones immune to electronic warfare. [Ukraine-Russia War]

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran accuses US of reckless military adventure amid Hormuz clashes
item.description: Iran FM Abbas Araghchi accused the US of opting for military action over diplomacy as US forces disabled two Iranian tankers and fired on more Iranian vessels. Qatari PM urges negotiations as ceasefire holds despite ongoing exchanges. [US-Iran War]

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary prepares for regime change as Magyar set to become PM
item.description: Péter Magyar is being sworn in as Hungary new prime minister after his Tisza party won a landslide victory, ending 16 years of Viktor Orbán rule. The new government inherits a grim economic situation and promises asset recovery investigations. [Europe]

item.title: 🇧🇭🇮🇷 Bahrain Arrests 41 People Linked to Iran's IRGC
item.description: Bahrain's interior ministry announced the arrest of 41 individuals it says were connected to Iran's Revolutionary Guards, amid heightened regional tensions following the US-Iran conflict. [Middle East]

item.title: 🎨⚔️ Venice Biennale Torn by Controversy Over Russian Participation
item.description: Russia's return to the Venice Biennale has sparked outrage, with Ukraine's culture minister comparing it to "inviting a serial killer to dinner with your friends" as the art world grapples with war's cultural impact. [Europe]

item.title: 🕊️ Zelenskiy confirms US-brokered three-day ceasefire and prisoner exchange with Russia
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy confirmed a three-day ceasefire arranged with Russia as part of US efforts to negotiate an end to the over four-year-old war. [Ukraine-Russia War]

item.title: 🇺🇸 Virginia Supreme Court throws out Democratic congressional map, boosts Republican midterm hopes
item.description: The court rejected a Democratic-backed ballot measure that reconfigured the state's US House districts for partisan advantage, dealing a major blow to Democratic midterm prospects. [US Politics]

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli artillery shells southern Lebanon, destroys homes in new strikes
item.description: Israeli forces continue targeting towns in southern Lebanon despite ceasefire arrangements, with artillery strikes hitting Braashit, Safad al-Battikh, and Touline amid ongoing truce violations. [Middle East]

item.title: 🇹🇷 Erdogan says Europe without Türkiye is incomplete and vulnerable in crisis management
item.description: Marking Europe Day, President Erdogan emphasized Turkiye as an essential partner for European security and crisis response, calling for a more inclusive EU amid global challenges. [Europe]

item.title: 🇫🇷 France seeks Africa reset at Kenya summit after West Africa setbacks
item.description: Paris aims to rebuild influence in Africa following losses in former colonies, co-hosting a regional summit in Kenya as part of a strategic pivot away from its troubled West African relationships. [Africa]

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine running critically low on air defence missiles after winter campaign
item.description: Ukraine's air force warned it is running short of air defence missiles following Russia's massive winter attack campaign, as Kyiv braces for further strikes amid ongoing conflict. [Ukraine-Russia War]

item.title: 💰 US trade chief says Trump administration will win appeal on temporary tariffs ruling
item.description: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said the administration expects to prevail in appealing the Court of International Trade's ruling against its temporary 10% global tariffs. [Economy]

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US and Iran Remain Deadlocked Despite Ongoing Gulf Clashes
item.description: Washington awaits Tehran's response to a US proposal to formally end the war, but neither side appears closer to resolution as military confrontations continue in the Strait of Hormuz. [US-Iran War]

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇸💼 US Warns EU of Higher Tariffs if Trade Deal Misses July 4 Deadline
item.description: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer stated that the US will revert to higher tariffs on European goods if Brussels fails to implement trade commitments by the July 4 deadline. [Economy]

item.title: 🇮🇳 India appoints new Chief of Defence Staff amid major military reforms
item.description: Retired Lt. Gen. NS Raja Subramani named India's next Chief of Defence Staff, succeeding Anil Chauhan as the country undertakes its biggest military overhaul to modernize and integrate the army, navy, and air force. [Defense Industry]
